label: 410nn–3 Advisory Commission

children.text: 3 members appointed for terms of 4 years from recommendations submitted by the National Maritime Museum Association.

2 members appointed for terms of 4 years from recommendations submitted by the Governor of the State of California, at least one of whom shall have professional expertise in maritime historic preservation.

4 members appointed for terms of 5 years from recommendations submitted by the Mayor of San Francisco with special consideration given to individuals with knowledge of museum and/or maritime issues and who represent the local fishing industry, recreational users, the business community, and neighborhood groups.
1 member appointed for a term of 5 years from recommendations from the Secretary of Commerce, who shall have professional expertise in the maritime industry.
2 members appointed for terms of 5 years, who shall have professional expertise in maritime history or historic preservation.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: b Compensation
children.text: Members of the Commission shall serve without pay. While away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Commission, members of the Commission shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in the same manner as persons employed intermittently in Government service are allowed expenses under .section 5703 of title 5

children.heading: Officers
children.label: c Officers
children.text: The Chair and other officers of the Commission shall be elected by a majority of the members of the Commission to serve for terms established by the Commission.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: d Meetings
children.text: The Commission shall meet at the call of the Chair or a majority of its members, but not less than twice annually. Seven members of the Commission shall constitute a quorum. Consistent with the public meeting requirements of chapter 10 of title 5, the Commission shall, from time to time, meet with persons concerned with maritime preservation.

children.heading: Bylaws and charter
children.label: e Bylaws and charter
children.text: The Commission may make such bylaws, rules, and regulations as it considers necessary to carry out its functions under this subchapter. The provisions of are hereby waived with respect to this Commission.section 1013(b) of title 5

children.heading: Functions
children.label: f Functions
children.text: The Commission shall advise the Secretary on the management and development of the park. The Secretary, or his designee, shall from time to time, but at least semiannually, meet and consult with the Commission on matters relating to the management and development of the park.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: g Termination
children.text: The Commission shall cease to exist 10 years after the date on which the first meeting of the Commission is held.
